briefing: Markets are mixed today with NASDAQ down 0.8% and S&P 500 flat — a neutral signal that doesn't justify deploying our remaining $577 in cash. Strategy memory is clear: don't force buys on neutral days just to deploy capital; wait for a genuine risk-on environment (NASDAQ +2%+) before adding new positions. All six existing positions remain intact with no stop-losses or profit targets triggered, so we hold and preserve dry powder for the next risk-on setup.

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Sector rotation is when investors shift money from one area of the market to another — for example, out of high-growth tech stocks (which drive the NASDAQ) and into more stable sectors like utilities or consumer staples (which make up a larger slice of the S&P 500). It explains why one index can fall while another holds flat on the same day. Recognizing rotation helps traders avoid chasing momentum in sectors that are quietly losing institutional support.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「板塊輪動」是指投資者將資金從市場的某一領域轉移至另一領域——例如從高成長科技股（推動納指的主力）流向公用事業或必需消費品等更穩定的板塊（在標普500中佔比較大）。這正是同一天納指下跌而標普持平的原因所在。識別板塊輪動，有助於交易者避免在機構資金悄然撤離的板塊中追漲。

market_education.summary_en: Today's session saw a split between major indices, with the S&P 500 finishing flat while the NASDAQ slipped 0.8%, signaling mild selling pressure concentrated in tech and growth stocks. No major macro catalysts drove a directional move, leaving markets in consolidation mode after a strong stretch of gains. This divergence between the S&P and NASDAQ typically reflects sector rotation — capital quietly shifting from high-multiple tech names into more defensive or value-oriented positions.
market_education.summary_zh: 今日市場走勢分化，標普500基本收平，而納指下跌0.8%，反映科技及成長股面臨溫和拋售壓力。市場缺乏明確的宏觀催化劑，在一段強勁升勢後進入整固階段。標普與納指的背離，通常意味著資金悄然從高估值科技股輪動至防禦性或價值型板塊。
